title: Quantum-Enhanced Competitive Game Learning: A Theoretical Framework
abstract: The integration of quantum computing principles into machine learning algorithms has the potential to revolutionize game strategy development. Building on the competitive approach to game learning, we explore how quantum algorithms can enhance the learning of strategies in complex games. We define a quantum-enhanced framework where quantum states and operations are utilized to represent and evolve game strategies. Our model leverages the principles of quantum superposition and entanglement to explore a vast strategy space more efficiently than classical methods. We introduce the concept of quantum teaching sets and quantum specification numbers, extending the classical notions to the quantum domain. Through theoretical analysis and simulations, we demonstrate that our quantum-enhanced competitive algorithm can solve games in a number of strategies polynomial in the logarithm of the strategy space sizes and the quantum specification number. We apply our framework to concept learning with a quantum counterexample oracle, showcasing its potential. Our complexity analysis reveals significant speedups over classical algorithms, opening new avenues for quantum-enhanced machine learning in game theory.
